message:
🌱 Add open-webui: feat: whole/full document mode mission (#1347)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,73 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "open-webui-3129-feat-whole-full-document-mode",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "open-webui: feat: whole/full document mode",
+    "description": "feat: whole/full document mode. Requested by 39+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current open-webui deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your open-webui version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui\nhelm list -n open-webui 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working open-webui installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review open-webui configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant open-webui configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui\nkubectl get configmap -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=open-webui\n```\nSometimes it may be ideal to pass the whole document to LLM for tasks like summarization. Currently, if you upload a document and ask the LLM to summarize it, the RAG would most likely just return no result."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for feat: whole/full document mode",
+        "description": "> Is it possible to bypass the current RAG system? I mean, having the documents inserted in the chat but opt to not use/apply them through the built-in RAG system.\n\nExample for Tools:\n\n```python\nclass Tools:\n    def __init__(self):\n        # If set to true it will prevent default RAG pipeline\n        self.file_handler = True\n```\nFrom: https://openwebui.com/t/hub/get_files\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui\nkubectl get events -n open-webui --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"feat: whole/full document mode\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "> Is it possible to bypass the current RAG system? I mean, having the documents inserted in the chat but opt to not use/apply them through the built-in RAG system.\n\nExample for Tools:\n\n```python\nclass Tools:\n    def __init__(self):\n        # If set to true it will prevent default RAG pipeline\n        self.file_handler = True\n```\nFrom: https://openwebui.com/t/hub/get_files",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "open-webui",
+      "community",
+      "ai-app",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "open-webui"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "community",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/issues/3129",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/pull/5744"
+    },
+    "reactions": 39,
+    "comments": 23,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],

+  },
